title: Poached pears and vanilla ice cream
excerpt: I call these my “tipsy pears”. They always look lovely, especially if you slice them lengthways and leave the little stem on. People think you're terribly fancy. I don't take any nonsense from an ice cream. You'll regularly find this in my freezer, for quickly pulling out for unexpected guests …

seo.title: Poached pears and vanilla ice cream - Marinda Kook

html:
I call these my “tipsy pears”. They always look lovely, especially if you slice them lengthways and leave the little stem on. People think you're terribly fancy.

I don't take any nonsense from an ice cream. You'll regularly find this in my freezer, for quickly pulling out for unexpected guests. You can stir in anything you like before you freeze it: chocolate shavings, cookie crumbs, marshmallows, dried fruit etc.

recipe.title: Poached pears and vanilla ice cream
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

directionGroups.title: Pears
steps: Put the red wine, orange peel, sugar, star anise, clove and cinnamon stick in a pot and heat until the sugar has dissolved.
Peel the pears, but keep the little stem on.
Put them into the red wine and let them simmer until the pears just turn soft.
Mix the cornflour with the water until a paste forms.
Stir it into the red wine and let the sauce thicken.
You can enjoy the pears warm or cold with the ice cream and the red wine sauce.

directionGroups.title: Ice cream
steps: Whip the cream and vanilla to soft peaks.
Pour the mixture into a container and freeze overnight.
